methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Ouros Energy is a Seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $3M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public reference (Lanzatech, LNZA), including frontier-tech leaders such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two methods anchor the valuation. The stage-progression method applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, using the $3M raised as a proxy baseline, yielding an implied valuation of $8M. The peer-median method examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A)—producing a median valuation of $150M and a range of $110M–$200M.

The triangulated analysis produces a low-to-high range of $6M–$172M, with a median of $79M across both methods. The significant spread reflects the inherent uncertainty in valuing early-stage frontier technology: stage-progression relies on limited operational history, while peer multiples are anchored to Series A valuations that may not yet reflect market-rate unit economics. Key uncertainties include technology maturity, capital efficiency relative to peers, and path to Series A funding. An interim Seed extension at $10M–$25M would better reflect capital needs and de-risk technical milestones before wider comp applicability.
